transcript: Medic 7 to County, coming in with a 71-year-old male, uh, c/o chest pain, onset about 20 minutes ago at rest. Hx of CAD, HTN, on metoprolol and aspirin. Allergic to penicillin. Vitals BP 158 over 94, HR 96, RR 18, sats 94%, GCS 15, alert, pain 8 out of 10. 12-lead shows ST elevation V2 through V4, transmitted en route. Gave 324 aspirin PO and 0.4 nitro SL. IV access established. Impression angina, possible STEMI. ETA 6 minutes.

pcr_json.events_leading: Patient was sitting at rest watching television when sudden onset chest pressure began approximately 20 minutes prior to EMS arrival.
